blocks.text: Continuously variable valve timing control (VTC) consists of a VTC actuator, VTC hydraulic valve, crank sensor, TDC sensor, and PGM-FI ECU. The PGM-FI ECU controls the VTC hydraulic valves on a duty cycle and applies hydraulic pressure to the advance or delay oil pressure chamber to optimize valve timing according to operating conditions (engine load, engine speed, and vehicle speed). If the current compared to the duty cycle output deviates from the set value, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status
If any of the following conditions persists for more than 5 seconds

items.text: VTC hydraulic valve current less than 0.20 A when duty output is greater than 40%.
When duty output is less than 5% and VTC hydraulic valve current is greater than 0.80A.
Ground fault in code (VTC line) between PGM-FI ECU and VTC hydraulic valve
Disconnection of cord (VTC line) between PGM-FI ECU and VTC hydraulic valve
Start the engine, run the radiator fan twice, and then warm up the engine for at least 2 minutes at 3,000 rpm with no load.
Vehicle speed [vehicle speed] 40 km/h-100 km/h for at least 5 seconds at a constant speed.
When conducting test drives, the driver must comply with all laws and regulations, observe good manners, and drive in accordance with the surrounding conditions.

blocks.text: Run the VTC test in the Inspection menu with HDS.
If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
